observations.recommendation:
1.17 We recommended and the Management agreed to direct:
a) The Inventory Committee to submit the Report on the Physical Count
of PPE, duly signed by authorized officials;
b) The Municipal Accountant, to undertake measures in locating
documents needed to provide the details of each PPE item, to indicate
the corresponding Property Item Number in PPE ledger cards and
conform with the prescribed format of the PPE ledger cards under
Annex 9, Volume II of the MNGAS for LGUs;
c) The GSO and Accounting Office to ensure the regular maintenance
of Property Cards (PC) and PPE Ledger Cards, respectively, for all PPE
items;
d) The Municipal Accountant to identify the unreconciled balance of the
Construction-in-Progress account, reclassify completed projects from
the Construction-in-Progress account to their appropriate PPE
accounts, and provide the corresponding depreciation;
e) The Municipal Accountant, to reclassify the tangible items below the
capitalization threshold of ₱50,000.00 to its appropriate account; and
f) The Municipal Accountant to undertake measures in identifying the
details of the PPE items without depreciation and make the necessary
adjusting entries in recording the depreciation of the respective PPE
items.
1.18 We also recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to implement the
one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as provided under COA Circular No.
2020-006 to finally establish the PPE balances that are verifiable as to
existence, condition and accountability.

2.10 We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the:
a) Inventory Committee to regularly conduct the physical count of
inventories and semi-expendable properties, prepare the corresponding
reports using the required format, and submit them to the Auditor
within the reglementary period;
b) General Services Officer to prepare the appropriate stock/property
cards and reconcile the supply/property records with the accounting
records;
c) Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Construction Materials
Inventory account and Fuel, Oil and Lubricants Inventory balance as
of December 31, 2024, and make the necessary adjusting entries to
reflect the correct balance as at year-end; and
d) General Services Officer to prepare the Monthly Report on Fuel
Consumption, using Appendix G of COA Circular No. 77-61 dated
September 26, 1977, and submit to the Audit team for verification.

3.7 We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a) Undertake measures in identifying the undetermined prior year's
balances of the account Due to NGAs;
b) Make appropriate adjusting entries to transfer special purpose funds to
the Trust Liabilities accounts and maintain a subsidiary ledger for each
fund;
c) Identify the cash prizes with or without specific utilization
guidelines/conditions imposed by the awarding body, and record them
accordingly, as either Miscellaneous Income or Trust Liabilities with the
necessary Prior Period Adjustments;
d) Submit the detailed schedule of fund transfers and utilization of the Due
to LGUs account for CY 2024; and
e) Reconcile the negative balance of the Due to LGUs subsidiary ledgers,
specifically for the LGSF-ADM on the project: Core Local Access Road in
Sitio 30, Barangay Nabitasan, amounting to ₱237,001.86.

4.17 We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a) Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to verify and adjust the
discrepancies noted and reconcile the cashbooks and accounting records
regularly so that cash account balances are accurately presented in the
financial statements;
b) Municipal Treasurer to prepare a Schedule of Unreleased Checks as at
year end and provide the same to the Municipal Accountant;
c) Municipal Accountant to issue a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) reverting
the unreleased checks as at year-end to the Cash in Bank-Local Currency,
Current Account based on the schedule provided by the Municipal
Treasurer;
d) Municipal Accountant to adjust the Cash in Bank- Local Currency,
Current Account and the corresponding liability/payable accounts
reported in the financial statements; and
e) Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the date of release of checks be
indicated in the Check Register and Disbursement Vouchers; and
f) Municipal Accountant to verify the negative balances of Cash Local
Treasury for Trust Fund and LEDO and provide supporting documents
and reports to ascertain its correctness.

5.8 We recommended and the Management agreed to ensure that the Municipal
Accountant:
a) Submit the Schedule of Accounts Payable as of December 31, 2024, for
verification purposes; and
b) Adhere to the provisions of Section 4 of P.D. No. 1445, COA Circular
No.2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 and the pertinent provisions of the
MNGAS for LGUs relative to the supporting documents necessary in the
recording of Accounts Payable of the Municipality and ensure that the
appropriate account are correctly used in the recording of the financial
transactions of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer:

To exert efforts to verify and validate the unreconciled balance of Due to LGUs amounting to ₱12.566 million and determine if there are pending projects for implementation and valid claims for the return or remittance of the idle funds; 

To remind the implementing department and end-users on the formulation of plans and programs of their dormant funds in the books of the Municipality; and 

Reconcile the records of Real Property Tax share due to LGUs between the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer so that the same will be remitted to valid claimants and likewise, for the constituents to avail of the benefits that can be derived therefrom.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the concerned officers to completely comply necessary documents by year-end for payment of suppliers/ creditors with valid claims to avoid unnecessary build-up of Accounts Payable balance.

We also recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Send confirmation letters for balances with identified claimants or creditors and process the payment with valid claims;

Make the necessary correcting entries pertaining to erroneous posting to the Accounts Payable account representing under payment of GSIS premiums of employees; and

Exert efforts to retrieve the available records and subsidiary ledgers of the Accounts Payable account in order to verify the validity of the unreconciled balances, otherwise, revert to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund the unliquidated balance of accounts payable in the books, which has been outstanding for two years or more and against which no actual claim, administrative or judicial, has been filed or which is not covered by perfected contracts on record.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to:

Require the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to provide the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year with the duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to serve as the basis for the set-up of the correct RPT/SET Receivables in the books; and

Direct the Municipal Accountant to review the recording of receivables and reconcile the accounting records with the Municipal Treasurer’s reports and immediately effect the necessary corrections and adjustments.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed that the Municipality make proper charges out of the DRRM fund taking into consideration the guidelines regarding the allocation and utilization of the fund provided for in DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 and other pertinent guidelines for LDRRMF for a more developed and improved disaster preparedness and response capabilities.


Likewise, we recommended that the Municipality formulate and implement DRRM programs, projects and activities that are congruent with the thematic areas and other policies of disaster risk reduction and management to strengthen and enhance the Municipality’s disaster capabilities at all levels.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to implement measures to collect garbage fees from residential establishments such as, conducting information drives and formulating the mechanics on the collection of fees, so that the revenue generating potential of the LGU’s Solid Waste Management Program will be optimized.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Health Officer and concerned officers to communicate with the DOH regarding the implementation of the remaining balance of the grant otherwise, return/remit the said amount to the source agency. 

We likewise recommended and the Municipal Health Officer agreed to submit copy of the MOA between the DOH CHD and the Municipality of Leganes to the Audit Team for proper evaluation of the implementation and distribution of the grant.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive and other concerned officials agreed to put forth greater efforts to fill the empty mandatory positions in the LDRRMO so that programs for disaster risk reduction management will be effectively and efficiently delivered.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and other concerned officials organize and fill-in the minimum required position classification and compensation scheme in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office as prescribed by NDRRMC, DILG, DBM and CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1, so that programs and projects pertaining to DRRM will be carried out efficiently and effectively.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive and other concerned officials agreed to ensure that the appropriation and utilization of the 20% DF is compliant with Item 3.2.5 of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 for the benefit of its constituents.

We recommended to the Management the following:

a.	Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to properly record the purchases of inventories in the appropriate inventory accounts and the consumption thereof as expense; and 

b.	Direct the concerned officials and employees to submit the Requisition and Issue Slips and Relief Distribution Sheets indicating the actual issuance and distribution of the welfare goods.
We recommended the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant make the necessary provision for depreciation of various PPE accounts.
We recommended that Management observe the basic controls in the receipt, recording, inventory, storage and distribution of the in-kind donations received.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require all concerned officers and employees to liquidate their long outstanding cash advances.  Demand letters should be sent to concerned officials and employees to liquidate immediately their cash advances. 

We likewise recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant require those who have unliquidated cash advance for travelling expenses to submit proof that their travel was actually consummated or if necessary, reconstruct the supporting documents and record the liquidation. This will allow the Audit Team to audit the liquidation and issue the proper Notice of Suspension or Disallowance or pass the subject transaction on audit based on our evaluation.  This must be done otherwise, the balances will just remain as unliquidated cash advances. 

We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Accountant stop granting additional cash advances to the concerned accountable officers unless their previous cash advances are settled and proper accounting thereof are made.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer, Market Supervisor and other officials concerned to enforce collection of the delinquent rentals including the applicable penalties and interest. 

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer and other officials concerned to strictly implement the guidelines set forth under the 2002 Municipal Revenue Code on the leasing of the market stalls and declare the stalls of non-paying occupants as vacant and be made available to qualified bidders.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GAD Focal Point System and Head of the implementing departments/offices to: 

Ensure the full implementation of the GAD programs, projects and activities in order to achieve the objective of women empowerment and gender equality and thus put into effect the gender mainstreaming strategy for the implementation of Republic Act No. 9710 or the Magna Carta of Women, and
Maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through implementation of the GAD related PPAs in order to attain the objective for which funds were provided.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the OIC- Municipal Accountant to validate/verify the balance of the Due from LGUs account amounting to    ₱1,474,487.17 and consider the possibility/comply with the requirements for the request for write-off if warranted or adjustment of the account balance from the Commission on Audit after due analysis/review.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to withhold the payment to the barangays or to request the recipient barangays to refund the payment received.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and officials concerned secure/formalize Municipality’s ownership over the property by having the same appraised and titled in its name. 

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to take up the Punong Banwa as Investment Property in the books of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive convene with the Local School Board and prepare their three-year program for the strategic prioritization of policies in the allocation and use of the Special Education Fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive through the Municipal Treasurer, LEDO and other concerned municipal officials look into all options to revive the revenue generation of the municipal fishponds as well as exercise all available remedies to put into effect the termination of the contracts and collect the delinquent fishpond rentals.

We recommended that Members of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) should submit the necessary documents showing compliance with the pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations and/or justify their departure from these prescribed guidelines.  Also requested are copies of the bidding documents made available to prospective bidders and the Official Receipts of bidders for purchase of these documents. 

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct concerned municipal officials to comply with the requirements of DILG Memorandum No. 2014-155 dated December 17, 2014 and/or justify their non-compliance with its requirements.
We requested the Local Chief Executive to furnish us a copy of the Municipality’s investigation and action on the matter.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned personnel to submit immediately the required documents enumerated above so that the correctness and validity of the procurement as well as the technical aspect could be evaluated accordingly.
We recommended that Management refund the CY 2015 Productivity Enhancement Incentive of ₱1,795,001.50 granted to elected officials, regular and casual employees.
We required the Municipal Engineer or the personnel in-charge of the project and other persons liable to refund the amount of                        ₱1,302,786.40.

We recommended that the municipal mayor see to it that disbursements out of the discretionary fund abide with the fundamental principles set forth in Section 4 of P.D.1445 or the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines to avoid suspension / disallowance in audit.

We likewise recommended the refund of the improperly disbursed funds totaling          ₱21,300.00.
We recommended the refund of the unnecessary and extravagant expenses incurred on a foreign travel.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the officials involved in the project to fully account for the disbursements made and to liquidate the same based on the guidelines set in the NGAS Manual for LGU’s.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the officials involved in the project to fully account for the disbursements made and to liquidate the same based on the guidelines set in the NGAS Manual for LGU’s, Vol. I.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the officials involved in the project to fully account for the disbursements made and to liquidate the same based on the guidelines set in the NGAS Manual for LGU’s.

We recommended that officials concerned submit the documents required under Section 3.1 of the Revised Guidelines and Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transactions prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001, dated June 14, 2012, to establish the validity of fund transfer.
We recommended for the immediate deposit of the cash in vault of the relieved Municipal Treasurer in compliance to Section 32 of the Manual of the New Government Accounting System, Volume I.

We recommended that the disallowed disbursements amounting to ₱143, 064.99 be refunded and the required documents enumerated in the Notice of Suspension No. 11-001-101(10) be submitted for the settlement of the suspended disbursements in order to avoid maturing into disallowance.

We recommended the following:

Immediate action should be undertaken to affect the issuance of Certificates of Title or Deeds of Donation and eventually the Certificates of Land Transfer to the name of the Municipality to preclude the occurrence of disputes over the use of property. 

The Municipal Mayor, being immediately responsible for the proper and effective use of these properties should assign one personnel to be directly responsible and accountable to him for all the documentary requirements and safekeeping of real property records. 

The Municipal Accountant, on the other hand, based on these papers, should book up all the donated lands and accordingly adjust the Land account to reflect duly documented properties owned by the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipality upon prior authority from the Sangguniang Bayan request authority from the Commission on Audit to write-off from the books of accounts the receivable from E.M. Projects. Once authority is granted, the Municipal Accountant should prepare the necessary Journal Entry Voucher with the following adjusting journal entry.

We recommended that Management facilitate execution of the Deed of Donation so that the Ambulance will be properly recorded in the books of accounts. 

As regard the odometer and speedometer, the same should be repaired in order to properly control and account gasoline consumption. Lastly, it is advised that the Accounting Office and Supply Officer reconcile their records for Motor Vehicles in order to arrive at the correct balance of the account Motor Vehicles (account code 241).

We recommended that the municipal officials and employees who received the extra cash gift for CY 2008 should be required to immediately refund the same. Compliance with Section 325 (a) of RA 7160 and LBC No. 75 is enjoined in order to avoid future suspensions/disallowances in audit.

1.21	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to direct the following:
Concerned officials to ensure the complete submission of RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor with balances duly reconciled with property cards and ledger cards;
Municipal Accountant and the OIC-GSO to update their respective PPELCs, CIPLCs, and PCs to record all procurement and disposals of PPEs so that a reconciliation with the physical count can be made; and
Municipal Accountant to exert efforts and devise plans on how to reflect the correct balances of PPE accounts that have been long recorded without depreciation so that the necessary journal entries could be made to book their respective depreciation and related accumulated depreciation, and make adjustments to Government Equity account.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to direct the following:
Municipal Accountant to reclassify all completed projects from Construction in Progress account to appropriate asset accounts and to take up the corresponding depreciation expenses of the completed projects from the time these were completed; and
Municipal Engineer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and the OIC-GSO, and provide them with necessary documents after project completion in order to periodically reconcile the status of the project with the accounting and GSO records.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Ensure further efforts to secure the documents supporting the prior years’ reconciling items, and cause prompt recording of the reconciling items, particularly those which persisted in the subsidiary ledgers for an extended period; and
Establish a systematic process for retaining necessary documents and ensure timely updates to subsidiary ledgers for Cash in Bank-Local Currency Cash Accounts (LCCA) to improve the reliability and accuracy of financial reporting.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to cause the preparation of adjusting entries of the negative balances/reconciling items with available records and ensure that transactions are analyzed before recording them in the books and subsequently file requests for authority to write off dormant fund transfers, if warranted.

5.11 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the following:
OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to strengthen the existing internal control measures over cash advances to ensure timely submission of liquidation documents and strictly monitor the AOs with outstanding cash advances;
OIC-Municipal Accountant to expedite the preparation of documents supporting the request for authority to write off dormant accounts; and
Various AOs, officers, and employees to liquidate their cash advances on time.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to review the recording of receivables, reconcile the accounting records with the Municipal Treasurer’s reports, and promptly implement the necessary corrections and adjustments.

8.7     We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
Direct the concerned official to submit the lacking supporting documents mentioned above, and
Strictly adhere to the provisions of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 on the grant of cash advance and liquidation of confidential funds.

9.11 We recommended and the Municipality agreed to strictly adhere to the guidelines outlined in relevant laws and regulations concerning the allocation and utilization of the fund designated for LDRRMF, to bolster the Municipality's capacity to prioritize and execute programs, projects, and activities geared towards community resilience and disaster preparedness.

10.8  We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to direct the following:
Municipal Engineer to prepare and attach the Inspection Report and Certificate of Completion for completed projects done by administration; and
Municipal Accountant, in collaboration with the Municipal Engineer, to undertake measures to ensure property management and monitoring, and attachment of relevant supporting documents relevant to the implementation of the Municipality’s development projects.

11.9   We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to:
Instruct the BAC to adhere strictly to procurement laws to ensure that correct procedures are followed when procuring goods and services and
Ensure that all municipal procurement transactions are undertaken through competitive bidding or alternative modes of procurement as provided under RA 9184.

12.7 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to review and discuss with the Sangguniang Bayan and Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) the possibility of amending the Municipal Ordinance in order to present doable rates and collection measures from residential establishments so that the revenue-generating potential of the LGU’s Solid Waste Management Program will be optimized.

13.8 	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive and other concerned officials agreed to ensure that the mandatory positions in the LDRRMO are filled to enhance its disaster risk reduction management programs significantly, thereby maximizing its capacity in disaster prevention, mitigation, preparedness, response, and recovery efforts.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure strict adherence with COA Circular No. 2012-002 in recording and booking-up of transactions regarding the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF).

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:

Adopt a written policy/procedure/guidelines in hiring Consultants/Contract of Service with a clearly defined scope of work;

Strictly comply with the provisions of COA-DBM Joint Circular (JC) No. 2, RA 9184 and CSC Resolution 020790, and ensure that these individuals are covered with contract of employment; and

Justify why the payment for these services shall not be disallowed in audit for failure to comply with the documentary requirements mandated by R.A. No. 9184 and for the supposed violation of CSC Resolution 020790.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to direct the:

Concerned Department Heads to see to it that DTRs are properly filled out by their respective job order personnel;

Municipal Accountant to thoroughly review the attached supporting documents for the payment of wages of job order personnel and see to it that the Authority to Render Overtime is attached to the disbursement voucher for those who render duty on weekends; and 

HRMO together with other Department Heads to draft a clear-cut policy regarding the hiring of job order personnel which includes the specific statement of duties and responsibilities in the job order contract and monitoring of the services rendered by the job order personnel.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to seek the services of the Provincial Legal Officer or Provincial Prosecutor in the absence of the Municipal Legal Officer to attend to its legal affairs, including representation in court.

We also recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to seek first a written conformity and acquiescence of the Solicitor General and the written concurrence of the Commission on Audit before hiring a private lawyer and strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 98-002 to avoid disallowance.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive and other concerned officials agreed to put forth more efforts in educating their constituents to avail the COVID-19 vaccines in the future.

We further recommended and the MHO and RHU agreed to plan properly the utilization and distribution of COVID-19 vaccines especially those nearing expiration to prevent wastages.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct concerned officials and employees to:

Submit the lacking supporting documents and ensure disbursements be covered with proper and complete documentations;

Revisit the BIR Ruling on the deduction withholding taxes on employee’s compensation and deduct withholding taxes on additional taxable compensation including overtime pay;  and

Comply with the requirements provided CSC-DBM JC No. 02 series of 2015 or Policies and Guidelines on Overtime Services and Overtime Pay for Government Employees to avoid disallowance.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the refund of disbursed funds amounting to ₱13,077.63 and henceforth, instruct the concerned officials and employees to ensure that government funds are spent for public purpose and for the betterment of the LGU.
We recommended strict adherence with the provisions of PD No. 1445 and RA No. 7160 regarding approval of disbursements of municipal funds.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Local Development Council and concerned municipal officials to formulate plans on how to effectively and efficiently implement the identified projects funded by the 20% DF and regularly monitor the status of implementation thereof.
We recommended that Management to instruct the officials concerned to see to it that the necessary documents to support the claims be thoroughly prepared and attached to the disbursement voucher.
We recommended the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit the lacking documents.

We recommended Management to direct the Acting LDRRMO to prepare the updated LDRRMFIP to cover items included in the approved supplemental budgets, and update the programs pertaining to its unexpended prior years LDRRMF in compliance with Item 5.0 COA of Circular No. 2012-002 and Item 6.0 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1.

We recommended that looking forward, the PPAs in the LDRRM Plan should be result-oriented. Expected Output/Performance Indicators should be quantifiable or measurable to ensure that the success or failure of the PPAs of the Municipality’s Disaster Risk Reduction Plan could be clearly established as well as to rationalize the allocation and utilization of resources.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to conduct additional tax campaigns to enforce the collection of the delinquent real property taxes, otherwise, avail of the remedies provided for in the Local Government Code.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the OIC- Municipal Accountant and other officials concerned to ensure that fund transfers be covered by a duly approved Memorandum of Agreement. 

We also recommended that the OIC- Municipal Accountant see to it that fund transfers are properly taken up in the books of the municipality and the barangay.
We recommended that Management submit the official receipts issued by beneficiary National High Schools.

We recommended that a Journal Entry Voucher taking up the unrecorded collections amounting to ₱241,705.91 be drawn and the corresponding amount be deposited by the Treasurer to the depository account of the municipality.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the:
Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entries to take up the depreciation for the current year, update the related Accumulated Depreciation account as well as record adjustments in the Government Equity Account for PY depreciation expenses not previously recorded; and
General Services Officer (GSO) to ensure timely submission of RPCPPE, conduct inventory of all unserviceable properties and prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) to facilitate and expedite the disposal of all unserviceable properties.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to Management thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2022-003 dated June 9, 2022.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the:
Municipal Engineer to validate projects with no details/schedules and issue Certificate of Completion of the validated projects to be used as basis by the Municipal Accountant in the reclassification to their PPE account;
Municipal Engineer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant after project validation/inspection so that the proper adjustments may be made to the CIP accounts; and
Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers for the CIP accounts, reclassify all completed projects from Construction in Progress account to appropriate asset accounts and to take up the corresponding depreciation expenses of the completed projects from the time these were completed until December 31, 2021.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to Management thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2022-006 dated June 13, 2022.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Enroll the Municipality’s accounts under the LBP weAccess facility inorder to obtain copy/ies of bank statements at the earliest possible time;
Review the previous years’ transactions, exhaust remedies to secure the documents supporting the prior years’ reconciling items and cause the immediate recording of the reconciling items which persisted in the BRS for a long period; and
Cause the submission of BRS under General Fund with Account Number Nos. 3292-1040-30 and 48.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to Management thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2022-004 dated June 10, 2022.

We recommended and the Municipality agreed to make proper charges out of the DRRM fund taking into consideration the guidelines regarding the allocation and utilization of the fund provided for in DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 and other pertinent guidelines of DRRM.
Likewise, we recommended and the Municipality agreed to formulate and implement DRRM programs, projects and activities that are congruent with the thematic areas and other policies of disaster risk reduction and management to strengthen and enhance the Municipality’s disaster capabilities at all levels.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to Management thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2022-008 dated June 15, 2022.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Direct the BAC to strictly comply with the procurement law so that proper procedures will be made on purchase of goods and services; and
Ensure that all procurement transactions of the Municipality be undertaken through competitive bidding or any alternative modes of procurement as provided under RA 9184.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to Management thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2022-007 dated June 13, 2022.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the following:
a.	The Municipal Accountant to exert efforts to verify and validate the unreconciled balance of Due to NGAs amounting to ₱1.896 million as of December 31, 2021 and determine if there are concerned Source Agency with valid claims in order to return/remit to them any unexpended/idle amount of fund transfer; and
b.	The concerned officers to periodically update the status of on-going and future projects with the accounting unit so that implementation of the PPAs will be properly monitored and well accounted for.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to Management thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2022-009 dated June 15, 2022.

We recommended and the Management and other concerned officials agreed to organize and fill-in the minimum required position classification and compensation scheme in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office as prescribed by NDRRMC, DILG, DBM and CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1, so that programs and projects pertaining to DRRM will be carried out efficiently and effectively.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to Management thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2022-002 dated June 9, 2022.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the concerned officials and employees to submit the lacking documents and strictly comply with all policy guidelines and regulations in the implementation of programs, projects and activities, and utilization of funds received for specific purposes to ensure the proper and judicious application of resources and to avoid disqualification on future projects and programs of the National Government.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to Management thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2022-005 dated June 10, 2022.

We recommended and the Management agreed to strictly adhere with the provisions of PD No. 1445 and RA No. 7160 regarding approval of disbursements of municipal funds.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to Management thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2022-010 dated June 15, 2022.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Local Development Council and concerned municipal officials to formulate plans on how to effectively and efficiently implement the identified projects funded by the 20% DF and regularly monitor the status of implementation thereof.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to Management thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2022-011 dated June 15, 2022.

We recommended and the Management and the Municipal Accountant agreed to submit the lacking/supporting documents.
We also recommended and the Management and all other LGU officials and employees agreed to exercise prudence, austerity, and restraint in the use of the Municipality’s resources especially in light of the current state of the country.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to direct the GSO:
To ensure timely submission of complete RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor;
To maintain complete, duly accomplished and updated PCs to record all procurement and disposals of PPEs so that a reconciliation between the PCs with the physical count can be made; and,
Together with the Municipal Accountant to conduct periodic reconciliation of the accounting and property records with the physical count and make the necessary adjustments for any discrepancies noted, for accurate and fair presentation of PPE account balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Review the previous years’ transactions, exhaust remedies to secure the documents supporting the prior years’ reconciling items and cause the immediate recording of the reconciling items which persisted in the BRS for a long period; and
Cause the submission of BRS under Special Education Fund with Account Number PVB 0026-003997-001. Review the previous years’ transactions, secure the documents supporting the prior years’ reconciling items and cause the immediate recording of the reconciling items particularly those which persisted in the BRS for a long period.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to properly record the purchases of inventories in the appropriate inventory accounts and the consumption thereof as expense; and
Direct the concerned officials and employees to submit the Requisition and Issue Slips and Relief Distribution Sheets indicating the actual issuance and distribution of the welfare goods.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned officials and employees to strictly comply with all policy guidelines and regulations in the implementation of programs, projects and activities, and utilization of funds received for specific purposes to ensure the proper and judicious application of resources and to avoid disqualification on future projects and programs of the National Government.
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant make the necessary provision for depreciation of various PPE accounts.

We recommended that Management:
